AD: A tiny mountain country with no airport — you have to drive in!
AE: Has the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa — over 800 meters high.
AF: Polo on horseback was probably invented somewhere around here.
AG: It has 365 beaches — one for every day of the year!
AI: People here race tiny wooden sailboats that they build by hand.
AL: Nodding your head means 'no' and shaking it means 'yes' here.
AM: One of the very first places in the world to make wine.
AO: Home to the giant sable antelope, found nowhere else on Earth.
AQ: Earth's southernmost continent — colder than a freezer, with hardly any people!
AR: The tango dance was born in this country.
AS: Most kids here grow up climbing coconut palms.
AT: Mozart was born here and started writing music at age 5!
AU: Kangaroos can hop faster than a car drives in a town.
AW: It almost never rains here, even though it's an island.
AX: Tiny Finnish islands where most people speak Swedish every day.
AZ: There are mountains here that shoot up actual fire from the ground.
BA: People here jump off a 500-year-old bridge for fun every summer.
BB: Singer Rihanna was born on this little Caribbean island!
BD: It's home to the Bengal tiger — one of the biggest cats on Earth.
BE: Belgian fries (with mayo!) were invented here, not in France.
BF: Its name means 'land of honest people' in two local languages.
BG: This country grows most of the rose petals used in fancy perfumes.
BH: It's a tiny country made of more than 30 islands joined by bridges.
BI: Kids here often herd long-horned cows that look like cartoons.
BJ: This is where the very first voodoo dolls came from.
BL: A tiny island once owned by Sweden, then bought by France!
BM: Famous for the 'Bermuda Triangle' — but mostly for pink sand beaches.
BN: The king lives in the world's biggest palace, with 1,788 rooms.
BO: It has a desert made of salt so big it's seen from space.
BQ: Three tiny tropical islands belonging to the Netherlands.
BR: The Amazon Rainforest makes about 20% of the world's oxygen.
BS: There are pigs that swim in the ocean here — really!
BT: This country measures success by happiness, not by money.
BW: Half of the country is covered by the giant Kalahari Desert.
BY: It's nicknamed 'the lungs of Europe' thanks to its huge ancient forest.
BZ: It has the world's second-biggest coral reef, full of sea turtles.
CA: It has more lakes than the rest of the world combined!
CC: Two dozen tiny islands shaped like a green ring in the sea.
CD: Home of the okapi — half-zebra, half-giraffe, all mystery animal.
CF: It has one of the world's biggest gatherings of forest elephants.
CG: Full of gorillas living in steamy jungles.
CH: Most of the world's chocolate companies started here.
CI: It grows the most cocoa beans on Earth — chocolate paradise!
CK: Children here learn to paddle traditional canoes before they can read.
CL: It has a desert where it didn't rain for 400 whole years.
CM: Soccer is so popular here, kids play barefoot on dirt fields every day.
CN: The Great Wall is so long it would stretch across Australia.
CO: Home of bright pink river dolphins that swim in the Amazon.
CR: It has more kinds of butterflies than the United States and Europe combined.
CU: Vintage 1950s American cars still roll around its streets every day.
CV: Ten green islands that rose out of the sea from old volcanoes.
CW: Famous for a bright blue liqueur named after the island.
CX: Every year, 50 million red crabs march across the roads to the sea.
CY: Legend says the goddess Aphrodite was born from sea foam here.
CZ: People here drink more beer per person than anywhere else in the world!
DE: More than 1,500 different kinds of sausage are made here!
DJ: It has a lake so salty you float on top without trying.
DK: LEGO bricks were invented here, in a tiny town called Billund.
DM: Nicknamed 'the nature island' — almost the whole place is rainforest.
DO: Baseball is so loved here, many MLB players come from this island.
DZ: About 80% of this country is covered by the giant Sahara Desert.
EC: You can stand right on the equator — one foot in each half of Earth.
EE: Skype, the video-call app, was invented in this little country.
EG: The Great Pyramid is older than woolly mammoths going extinct.
EH: A huge sandy area with more camels than people.
ER: Old Italian-style coffee shops are still everywhere in its capital.
ES: People here have a famous tomato fight every August!
ET: This is where coffee was first discovered, by a goat herder!
FI: It has more saunas than cars — almost one for every three people.
FJ: Made of more than 300 islands, but only about 100 have people on them.
FK: There are about 200 sheep for every single person who lives here!
FM: Tiny island nation where ancient stone 'money' can be bigger than a kid.
FO: Sheep outnumber people here, and the grass-roof houses are real.
FR: The Eiffel Tower can grow about 15 cm taller in summer heat.
GA: Almost 90% of this country is covered in thick rainforest.
GB: Made of four countries — England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland — joined as one!
GB-ENG: Big Ben is the nickname of a giant bell, not the clock tower.
GB-NIR: The Giant's Causeway has 40,000 stone columns made by an ancient volcano.
GB-SCT: Has more castles than any other country in Europe — over 2,000 of them!
GB-WLS: The flag has a real red dragon on it — the only flag in the UK with a dragon!
GD: Called 'The Spice Island' because it grows so much nutmeg and cinnamon.
GE: People here have been making wine for over 8,000 years!
GF: Europe launches space rockets from a jungle base here.
GG: A tiny island that uses its own kind of pound coins.
GH: Home to Lake Volta, the biggest human-made lake in the world by area.
GI: Wild monkeys called Barbary macaques live on this giant rock!
GL: It's mostly covered in ice — the biggest island on the planet.
GM: A skinny little country wrapped almost completely around a long river.
GN: Bauxite from here ends up in soda cans all over the world.
GP: A Caribbean island shaped exactly like a butterfly from the sky.
GQ: The only country in Africa where Spanish is the main language.
GR: The very first Olympic Games happened here almost 3,000 years ago!
GS: Penguin colonies here can have a million birds in one place.
GT: Its quetzal bird's tail feathers can be longer than a baseball bat.
GU: A small Pacific island where palm trees show up on the license plates.
GW: Has 88 tiny islands, many with no people — just monkeys!
GY: Home to Kaieteur Falls, about five times taller than Niagara Falls.
HK: Its skyline has more skyscrapers than anywhere else on Earth.
HN: It once rained fish from the sky here — and people still talk about it.
HR: The necktie was invented here, by Croatian soldiers long ago.
HT: The very first country where slaves freed themselves and made a new nation.
HU: The Rubik's Cube was invented here in 1974!
ID: Made up of more than 17,000 islands — count them if you can.
IE: It's nicknamed 'The Emerald Isle' because the grass is super green.
IL: Has the saltiest sea on Earth — the Dead Sea, where you just float!
IM: Has a famous tailless cat breed called the Manx.
IN: Chess was invented here a very, very long time ago.
IO: Tiny coral atolls full of sea turtles and almost no people.
IQ: Writing was invented here over 5,000 years ago — on clay tablets!
IR: It's home to one of the oldest continually-lived-in cities in the world.
IS: Has more than 100 volcanoes, and many of them are still active!
IT: Pizza was invented in Naples over 200 years ago.
JE: The cute 'Jersey' cow breed comes from this small island.
JM: Bob Marley, the king of reggae music, came from this island.
JO: A whole city was carved into pink rock here — called Petra.
JP: There are more vending machines than people in some neighborhoods.
KE: Lions, elephants, and giraffes all share giant grasslands called savannas.
KG: Most of this country is covered in tall, snowy mountains.
KH: Has the world's biggest religious building — Angkor Wat temple.
KI: One of the first countries to see each new sunrise on Earth.
KM: Tiny islands where ylang-ylang flowers go into fancy perfume.
KN: Two volcanic islands joined by a strip of beach.
KP: People here use a different calendar that starts from 1912.
KR: K-pop, smartphones, and tasty kimchi all come from here.
KW: It hardly ever rains here — sometimes only a few drops the whole year.
KY: The water is so clear, you can see fish 30 meters below the surface.
KZ: The biggest country in the world that has no ocean coast.
LA: A landlocked country covered with elephants — friendly ones!
LB: Old cedar trees here are mentioned in the Bible — really, really old.
LC: Has two giant pointy mountains called the Pitons popping out of the sea.
LI: One of the only countries with a prince who actually rules it!
LK: The world's first country to have a woman as prime minister.
LR: Founded by people from the United States who used to be slaves.
LS: A whole country surrounded by another country (South Africa)!
LT: It once had the biggest empire in all of Europe.
LU: This tiny country has the world's only Grand Duke as its ruler.
LV: People here celebrate the longest day of summer with bonfires all night.
LY: Most of the country is desert, and water comes from underground rivers.
MA: Has goats that climb up trees to eat fruit — really!
MC: The whole country is smaller than New York City's Central Park.
MD: One of the world's biggest underground wine cellars is here.
ME: Its name means 'black mountain' — the mountains do look dark!
MF: One island shared by France and the Netherlands at the same time.
MG: Lemurs live here and nowhere else on Earth.
MH: Has more lagoon area than land — water is everywhere.
MK: Mother Teresa was born here, in the city of Skopje.
ML: Has a city, Timbuktu, that used to be one of the world's richest places.
MM: Has a giant golden temple covered in 2,000 diamonds.
MN: Has more horses than people, and many kids ride before they walk!
MP: Tropical islands where it's never colder than warm.
MQ: A tropical French island where everyone speaks French and Creole.
MR: Has trains 2 km long carrying iron through the desert.
MS: Half of this island is off-limits because of an active volcano!
MT: The whole country fits inside one fairly small city.
MU: The dodo bird used to live here, before it went extinct.
MV: Made of tiny coral islands, none of them more than 2 meters above the sea.
MW: Has a giant freshwater lake full of more fish kinds than any other lake.
MX: Chocolate was invented here, by the ancient Mayans.
MY: Two giant towers here used to be the tallest buildings in the world.
MZ: Its flag is the only one in the world with a real rifle drawn on it.
NA: Has sand dunes as tall as skyscrapers in its desert.
NC: A French Pacific island known for its rare blue-tongued lizards.
NE: One of the hottest countries in the world, year-round.
NF: A tiny green island in the Pacific with its own special pine tree.
NG: The most people of any country in Africa — over 200 million!
NI: Has freshwater bull sharks living in a lake — yes, real sharks!
NL: People here own more bicycles than the country has people.
NO: Some places here have sun all night for weeks every summer.
NP: Mount Everest, the world's tallest mountain, is here.
NR: The third-smallest country on Earth — you can drive around it in 30 minutes.
NU: One big coral rock island, with only about 1,600 people.
NZ: The kiwi, a chicken-sized bird that can't fly, lives only here.
OM: Has frankincense trees — the same kind mentioned in old stories.
PA: A canal here lets giant ships cut between two oceans.
PE: Llamas and alpacas roam here in the high Andes mountains.
PF: A hundred and eighteen islands and atolls scattered across the South Pacific.
PG: People here speak more than 800 different languages.
PH: Made of more than 7,000 tropical islands.
PK: Has the highest paved road in the world — way up in the Karakoram mountains.
PL: Has a forest where every tree grows with a giant curve at the bottom.
PM: A tiny chunk of France right next to Canada.
PN: Only about 50 people live here — one of the world's smallest groups.
PR: Has a bay that glows blue in the dark from tiny sea creatures.
PS: Olive trees here are some of the oldest in the world.
PT: Sailors from this country mapped out much of the world long ago.
PW: It has a lake full of harmless jellyfish you can swim with.
PY: There are TWO official languages here, and almost everyone speaks both.
QA: One of the hottest countries, but has malls big enough for indoor snow!
RE: A French island with one of the most active volcanoes on Earth.
RO: Home of Bran Castle, which inspired the story of Dracula!
RU: The biggest country on Earth — it spans 11 different time zones!
RW: Half of all mountain gorillas in the world live here.
SA: Most of it is covered by hot, sandy desert.
SB: Has nearly 1,000 islands, many with their own language.
SC: Home to the world's heaviest seed — the giant coco de mer.
SD: Has more pyramids than Egypt — about 200 of them!
SE: People here invented the seatbelt to keep families safe in cars.
SG: A whole country that fits inside one big city.
SH: One of the most remote islands on Earth — Napoleon was sent here.
SI: More than half of this country is covered by forests.
SJ: Polar bears here outnumber the people who live there.
SK: Has more castles per person than anywhere else in the world.
SL: Diamonds are found in rivers here — sometimes just sitting in the riverbed.
SM: One of the smallest and oldest countries in the world.
SN: Has giant baobab trees so big you can fit dozens of kids inside the trunk.
SO: Has the longest coastline of any African country — lots of beaches!
SR: About 90% of this country is covered in untouched rainforest.
SS: The world's youngest country — born in 2011.
ST: Two volcanic islands with cocoa farms everywhere.
SV: It was the first country to make Bitcoin legal money.
SX: Planes land so close to the beach, hats blow off!
SY: Damascus, its capital, may be the world's oldest still-lived-in city.
SZ: One of the few countries left with a real king who rules.
TC: Forty tiny islands with some of the clearest water on Earth.
TD: Has a lake that has shrunk to a tenth of its old size — sand keeps growing!
TF: A penguin's paradise — way down near Antarctica.
TG: A long, narrow country with markets full of colorful fabrics.
TH: White elephants are considered super lucky here.
TK: A tiny island country that runs on 100% solar power.
TL: One of the youngest countries in Asia — born in 2002.
TM: Has a giant gas pit in the desert that has been on fire since 1971.
TN: Many Star Wars desert scenes were filmed in this country!
TO: One of the only Pacific kingdoms that was never ruled by Europe.
TR: Has tulips that came here long before they reached the Netherlands.
TT: Steel drums — those bouncy band drums — were invented here!
TV: One of the world's lowest countries — barely above the ocean.
TW: Bubble tea, the chewy-pearl drink, was invented here.
TZ: Mount Kilimanjaro, the tallest mountain in Africa, is here.
UA: Has the deepest metro station in the world — about 105 meters underground.
UG: About half of all mountain gorillas in the world live here too.
UM: Tiny specks of US land scattered across the Pacific Ocean.
US: It has the world's longest cave system in Kentucky.
UY: Almost all of this country's electricity comes from wind and water.
UZ: Has gorgeous blue-domed mosques along the old Silk Road.
VA: The smallest country in the world — smaller than a stadium!
VC: Thirty-something islands, many with no roads — only boats.
VE: Angel Falls here is the tallest waterfall on Earth — almost a kilometer down.
VG: Sixty islands, perfect for sailing pirate-style adventures.
VI: A US territory where people drive on the LEFT side of the road.
VN: People here zip around on more than 50 million motorbikes!
VU: Eighty volcanic islands, some of which still erupt today.
WF: Three tiny French islands with three actual kings.
WS: People here can have tattoos that cover almost their whole body.
XK: One of the world's youngest countries — only declared independence in 2008.
YE: Has buildings made of mud that are seven stories tall and 500 years old!
YT: A French island in the Indian Ocean with coral lagoons full of dolphins.
ZA: It has three capital cities — not just one!
ZM: Half of Victoria Falls — the biggest curtain of water on Earth — is here.
ZW: Home to ruins of an ancient stone city no one knows who built.
